title: FK-direction conservative loss

queries: graph prompt alignment direction preservation foreign key role asymmetry llm
reconstructive graph token alignment conservative role-aware relation direction supervision

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: The central bottleneck is graph-text alignment quality rather than prompt wording, so structural invariants should be enforced where graph states are projected into prompt space.
papers.relevance: Supports targeting token-query alignment with a local conservative penalty instead of another prompt-side or coefficient-only scan.

papers.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
papers.claim: Explicit graph-side supervision improves alignment, but stronger global reconstruction can be too blunt if the useful invariant is more local than whole-graph recovery.
papers.relevance: Supports replacing graph-global pressure with a narrower direction-preservation objective at the token-query alignment layer.

papers.title: Graph-Aware Language Model Pre-Training on a Large Graph Corpus Can Help Multiple Graph Applications
papers.claim: Graph-aware supervisory signals can improve cross-task transfer when the auxiliary objective preserves structurally meaningful graph information.
papers.relevance: Supports testing a structure-preserving finetune auxiliary loss that is narrower and safer than the blocked pretrain path.

evidence.why_now: EXP060 and EXP063 showed that stronger local alignment pressure can help user-churn, but graph-global reconstruction still regresses user-ltv and item-incoterms. The next lower-risk branch is therefore a more local conservative loss that preserves source-vs-target role asymmetry along foreign-key routes without introducing graph-global pressure.

hypothesis: Current basis BCE and reconstruction losses can still blur source-vs-target role information because both sides of an FK route are optimized mainly toward shared pair-level semantics. Adding a modest token-query penalty when FK-source and FK-target alignment states collapse toward high cosine similarity should preserve join-role readability, retain the recent classification-side gains from stronger alignment, and reduce the regression and salt-side damage caused by graph-global reconstruction.
notes: This is a finetune-only route-aware conservative loss candidate; do not use pretrain or --pretrain.
Implementation scope is intentionally isolated: add only one FK-direction conservative term on top of the refreshed 512-step screening baseline.
Chosen GPUs reflect the currently idle set observed from the local orchestrator on 2026-05-07; do not restore the old fixed 5/6/7 rule.
Launched on 2026-05-07 after syncing the current main.py/model.py candidate patch plus generated wrappers and launcher to the remote repo and /tmp.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.
user-churn improved, but user-ltv and item-incoterms both regressed against the refreshed 512-step strict baselines.
